mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of months following a rate cut, or rolling Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 7.0 percent, consumer spending power deteriorates sharply, causing corporate earnings revisions to turn negative and driving S&P 500 returns down over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in >60% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 7.0, or the signal shows zero predictive power in out-of-sample backtests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts signals broad-based domestic demand growth, which translates into higher corporate revenues and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns when RSAFS and HOUST both rise, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days specifically during periods of stable inflation.
failure_condition: The signal reverses direction or becomes statistically insignificant when CPIAUCSL month-over-month growth exceeds 0.5 percent, or Rank IC drops below 0.01 in stable inflation regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect expanding economic activity and rising household income, which consistently generate positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >50% of months following positive payroll revisions, or the rolling correlation between payroll changes and short-term returns falls below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index surpasses 230.0, inflation expectations accelerate and force monetary authorities to tighten policy, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 60-day returns in >65% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds 230.0, or the threshold effect vanishes in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in real GDP and real personal consumption expenditures confirms synchronized macroeconomic expansion, which reliably produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields negative 20-day returns in >55% of quarters where both series expand, or the interaction coefficient becomes insignificant in predictive models.

mechanism_narrative: Declines in industrial production signal weakening manufacturing demand, which drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days exclusively during periods of rising unemployment.
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict negative 5-day returns when UNRATE is rising, or the predictive power disappears in regimes where unemployment is stable or falling.
